creator: Cy.Schubert@komquats.com
text:
I bet this is a permissions issue. (I tested it on my testbed outside of a 
jail on on my prod system within a jail -- same result, it worked.) AFAIC 
there is no problem with WRKDIRPREFIX with the port.


-- 
Cheers,
Cy Schubert <Cy.Schubert@komquats.com>
FreeBSD UNIX:  <cy@FreeBSD.org>   Web:  http://www.FreeBSD.org

			e**(i*pi)+1=0


In message <CAF6927E-8BCB-4363-B7A1-556346172E72@gmx.net>, Oliver Loch 
writes:
> Hi guys,
> 
> sorry for the late reply, but I was on a business trip and I'm now in the mid
> dle of moving to a different county ...
> 
> I did what Olli mentioned:
> 
> [root@foo /basejail/usr/ports/sysutils/screen]# make -V WRKDIRPREFIX
> /var/ports
> [root@foo /basejail/usr/ports/sysutils/screen]# 
> 
> But it didn't change anything:
> 
> [root@foo /basejail/usr/ports/sysutils/screen]# make package
> ===>  Building package for screen-4.0.3_9
> tar: Failed to open '/basejail/usr/ports/sysutils/screen/screen-4.0.3_9.tbz'
> pkg_create: make_dist: tar command failed with code 256
> *** Error code 1
> 
> Stop in /basejail/usr/ports/sysutils/screen.
> *** Error code 1
> 
> Stop in /basejail/usr/ports/sysutils/screen.
> [root@foo /basejail/usr/ports/sysutils/screen]# 
> 
> As the posted patch is not addressing the problem, I haven't applied it. If r
> equested again, I'll do.
> 
> Cya
> 
> KR,
> 
> Oliver
> 
> Am 09.01.2011 um 17:04 schrieb olli hauer:
> 
> > On 2011-01-09 08:08, Cy Schubert wrote:
> >> Unable to reproduce the problem locally.
> >> 
> >> The posted patch does not address the problem
> >> 
> > 
> > Sorry, yes WRKDIRPREFIX is also working for me.
> > Don't know why but I read only PREFIX instead of WRKDIRPREFIX.
> > 
> > Maybe the submitter can show us the output of
> > # make -V WRKDIRPREFIX
> > inside the screen port directory.
> > 
> > The patch I submitted allows builds with a custom PREFIX, so
> > tools like ports-mgmt/genplist can work with this port.
>
